title: Fix off-by-one error in pagination
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Uses Math.ceil (not Math.floor, not Math.round, not manual ternary). Deduct 2 if wrong rounding method.
- [ ] Fix is minimal — only the totalPages line changed. Deduct 1 per unnecessary modification to other lines.
- [ ] No new parameters, no renamed variables, no added features. Deduct 1 if scope creep.
- [ ] Function signature unchanged. Deduct 2 if signature changed.
- [ ] Handles pageSize=0 gracefully OR leaves it unchanged (both acceptable). Do not deduct for ignoring this edge case.

title: Fix async race condition in data fetching
expected_fix: Add AbortController or a boolean cleanup flag in useEffect return
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] useEffect returns a cleanup function. Deduct 3 if no cleanup return.
- [ ] Uses AbortController (preferred) OR a boolean flag (acceptable). Deduct 2 if neither.
- [ ] If AbortController: signal is passed to fetch AND .abort() is called in cleanup. Deduct 2 if signal missing.
- [ ] If boolean flag: flag is set in cleanup AND setState is guarded by flag check. Deduct 2 if guard missing.
- [ ] Does NOT swallow errors silently — catch block should exist for AbortError or the flag check. Deduct 1 if no error handling.
- [ ] Preserves original functionality (still fetches by userId, still calls setUser). Deduct 2 if core behavior changed.
- [ ] No unnecessary state additions (e.g. adding loading/error state not requested). Deduct 1 for scope creep.

title: Fix incorrect date comparison in timezone handling
expected_fix: Replace getUTC* methods with getFullYear, getMonth, getDate (local time)
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] All three getUTC* methods replaced with local equivalents (getFullYear, getMonth, getDate). Deduct 3 if any getUTC* remains.
- [ ] Does NOT use toLocaleDateString() or toDateString() string comparison (fragile, locale-dependent). Deduct 1 if string-based comparison used.
- [ ] Fix is minimal — only the comparison methods changed. Deduct 1 per unnecessary modification.
- [ ] Function signature unchanged. Deduct 2 if signature changed.
- [ ] Does NOT introduce timezone library (moment, date-fns, luxon) for this simple fix. Deduct 1 for over-engineering.

title: Refactor repeated fetch logic into a reusable service
expected_fix: Generic ApiService class with a typed get<T>(endpoint: string) method
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Defines a class (e.g. ApiService) with a generic method like get<T>(endpoint): Promise<T>. Deduct 2 if no generics.
- [ ] Authorization header logic appears exactly ONCE in the codebase. Deduct 2 if duplicated.
- [ ] Error handling (res.ok check + throw) appears exactly ONCE. Deduct 1 if duplicated.
- [ ] getToken() is called inside the shared method, not passed as a parameter. Deduct 1 if caller must pass token.
- [ ] The three original functions (getUsers, getOrders, getProducts) are either removed or reimplemented as thin wrappers calling the shared method. Deduct 1 if original functions left as-is.
- [ ] No unnecessary abstractions (e.g. adding interceptors, middleware, request builders not asked for). Deduct 1 for over-engineering.

title: Replace deeply nested conditionals with strategy pattern
expected_fix: A Record<string, string[]> lookup map or a Map, replacing all branching logic
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Zero nested else-if chains remain. Deduct 3 if any nesting persists.
- [ ] Uses a data structure (Record, Map, object literal) instead of branching. Deduct 2 if uses switch/case or flat if-else chain (these eliminate nesting but aren't a lookup pattern).
- [ ] All 4 roles (admin, editor, viewer, moderator) produce the exact same permissions as the original. Deduct 3 per incorrect role mapping.
- [ ] Unknown roles return empty array []. Deduct 2 if returns undefined, null, or throws.
- [ ] Uses TypeScript typing (e.g. Record<string, string[]>). Deduct 1 if untyped.
- [ ] Adding a new role requires only adding one line to the data structure. Deduct 1 if adding a role requires more changes.

title: Refactor callback pyramid into async/await
expected_fix: async/await version with try/catch error handling
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Function is declared async and returns a Promise. Deduct 3 if still uses callback parameter.
- [ ] Uses await for getUser, getOrders, getProducts — no .then() chains. Deduct 1 if uses .then().
- [ ] Has try/catch for error handling. Deduct 2 if no error handling.
- [ ] Error propagation works — errors from any of the three calls should bubble up. Deduct 2 if errors are swallowed.
- [ ] Preserves the data flow: getUser(userId) → getOrders(user.id) → getProducts(orders[0].id) → return combined. Deduct 3 if data flow broken.
- [ ] No callback parameter in the function signature. Deduct 2 if callback still present.
- [ ] catch block re-throws or lets error propagate (not: catch and return null/undefined). Deduct 1 if errors silenced.

title: Implement a typed REST API client with retry logic
prompt:
Write a TypeScript function `fetchWithRetry<T>(url: string, options: RequestInit, maxRetries: number): Promise<T>` that:
1. Fetches the given URL
2. On network error or 5xx response, retries up to maxRetries times with exponential backoff (100ms, 200ms, 400ms...)
3. On 4xx error, throws immediately without retrying
4. Returns the parsed JSON response typed as T
expected_fix: fetchWithRetry implementation with exponential backoff and 4xx/5xx distinction
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Exponential backoff: delay doubles each retry (e.g. 100, 200, 400). Deduct 1 if linear or fixed delay. Deduct 1 if base/multiplier is wrong but pattern is exponential.
- [ ] 4xx errors throw IMMEDIATELY with no retry. Deduct 1 if 4xx triggers retry.
- [ ] 5xx errors trigger retry. Deduct 1 if 5xx treated same as 4xx.
- [ ] Network errors (fetch throws) also trigger retry. Deduct 1 if only HTTP status codes handled.
- [ ] Respects maxRetries — does not retry more than maxRetries times. Deduct 1 if off-by-one (e.g. retries maxRetries+1 times).
- [ ] Function signature uses generic <T> and returns Promise<T>. Deduct 1 if returns Promise<any>.
- [ ] Delay uses setTimeout wrapped in a Promise (or equivalent). Deduct 1 if uses blocking delay or no delay at all.
- [ ] Throws after exhausting retries (does not return undefined/null). Deduct 1 if fails silently.

title: Add request deduplication to an API client
expected_fix: Check Map for existing promise, return it if found, otherwise create/store/cleanup new request
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Checks the Map for an existing in-flight Promise before calling fetch. Deduct 3 if always fetches.
- [ ] Returns the SAME Promise instance for concurrent requests to the same URL (not a new Promise wrapping the old one). Deduct 2 if creates wrapper promises.
- [ ] Cleanup (Map.delete) is in a .finally() block so it runs on BOTH success and failure. Deduct 2 if only cleans up on success. Deduct 3 if never cleans up.
- [ ] After cleanup, a new request to the same URL creates a fresh fetch. Deduct 2 if permanently caches.
- [ ] Different URLs are fetched independently. Deduct 3 if all URLs share one entry.
- [ ] Generic <T> typing preserved on the function signature. Deduct 1 if returns any.
- [ ] Does NOT store resolved data — only stores the in-flight Promise. Deduct 1 if caches results beyond the request lifecycle.

title: Parse and normalize a paginated API response
expected_fix: Loop using next_cursor, accumulate all T[] items, stop when cursor is null
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] Loops until next_cursor is null (not undefined, not falsy — specifically null). Deduct 1 if uses loose falsy check (could break on cursor='0').
- [ ] Accumulates all items from every page into a single array. Deduct 3 if only returns last page.
- [ ] Passes headers to every fetch call, not just the first. Deduct 2 if headers dropped on subsequent pages.
- [ ] Cursor is appended as a query parameter (e.g. ?cursor=xxx). Deduct 2 if cursor passed in body or header.
- [ ] First request does NOT include a cursor parameter. Deduct 1 if sends cursor=null or cursor=undefined on first request.
- [ ] Function signature uses generic <T> and returns Promise<T[]>. Deduct 1 if returns any[].
- [ ] No infinite loop protection is acceptable for easy difficulty, but bonus if included (e.g. max page limit).

title: Implement a simple in-memory request cache with TTL
expected_fix: Check cache for unexpired entry, return it if valid, otherwise fetch and store with expiresAt = Date.now() + ttlMs
judge_criteria:
Check each:
- [ ] TTL check compares Date.now() against stored expiresAt. Deduct 2 if comparison is inverted (e.g. Date.now() > expiresAt means expired, not valid).
- [ ] Cache stores expiresAt as Date.now() + ttlMs at write time. Deduct 2 if TTL calculated incorrectly.
- [ ] Returns cached data WITHOUT calling fetch when cache is valid. Deduct 3 if always fetches.
- [ ] Calls fetch and updates cache when entry is missing or expired. Deduct 2 if stale data returned forever.
- [ ] Uses the provided Map and CacheEntry type from the starter code. Deduct 1 if reinvents the cache structure.
- [ ] Generic <T> typing preserved. Deduct 1 if returns any.
- [ ] Does NOT set a timer/interval to evict entries (lazy expiration is correct for this task). Deduct 1 for unnecessary background cleanup.
